description_long: This is a large, L-shaped reception or service counter with a dark woodgrain top and a body clad in vertically textured gray panels. The front face features three large, black rectangular insets, likely decorative or possibly for signage. The countertop has a raised section along one side, providing a privacy barrier or transaction ledge, and a cutout at the corner for cable management or access. The rear side is open, with a recessed workspace and a lower shelf or modesty panel, allowing for storage or legroom. The overall construction appears robust, suitable for high-traffic urban environments such as lobbies, offices, or public service areas.
description: A large, L-shaped reception counter with a dark woodgrain top, gray textured panels, and black insets, featuring a raised privacy ledge and open rear workspace.
description_view_0: Front view showing three black rectangular insets on the gray panel and the full length of the woodgrain countertop.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the counter, a single black inset, and the thickness of the wood top.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the open workspace, lower shelf, and the L-shaped configuration of the counter.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the raised privacy ledge and the woodgrain surface.
